title: Five rules that say nothing
checkLabels.t14: The heading is still dark red
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. The five rules are still silent.
hint2.untouched: You only work in styles.css. The rules are there, none of them matches.
why.not-closed: A closing brace, quote or square bracket is missing.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.lost-items: There are no longer four items.
why.lost-p: There are no longer two paragraphs.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. Here you fix the CSS, not the document.
hint2.touched-html: The three classes in the HTML are the correct ones: intro, menu, note. The mistakes are in the other file.
hint3.touched-html: The temptation is to rewrite the HTML until it matches the CSS. On a real site the HTML comes from somewhere else and is not yours to move. You fix the rule.
why.id-rule-left: The rule with the hash is still there. The list has no id, it has a class.
hint2.id-rule-left: A hash looks for an id. The HTML says class. One character apart.
why.no-intro: There is no paragraph with the class intro.
why.intro-silent: The first paragraph is not teal. Its rule says nothing.
hint2.intro-silent: Compare the class name in the CSS with the one in the HTML. Letter by letter.
hint3.intro-silent: Class names are case-sensitive. Intro and intro are two different names — the browser does not guess what you meant, and it does not complain.
why.no-menu: There is no list with the class menu.
hint2.menu-silent: Look at the character the rule starts with and the attribute in the HTML.
why.no-note: There is no item with the class note.
why.note-silent: The tax note is not italic.
hint2.note-silent: There is a space between li and .note. It means something.
hint3.note-silent: With a space, the rule looks for an element with the class note INSIDE the item. There is none — the class is on the item itself. Without the space, both conditions describe the same element.
why.no-items: There is no list item at all.
why.arrow-silent: There is no arrow before the items, even though the rule is there.
hint2.arrow-silent: Look at the value of content. Compare it with what you wrote in lesson 14.
hint3.arrow-silent: Text in content goes in quotes. Without them the value is invalid and the browser throws the whole declaration away — quietly, as it does with every value it cannot read.
why.no-link: There is no link to another site.
why.link-silent: The outside link is not bold.
hint2.link-silent: The rule asks whether href IS http. It is a whole address.
hint3.link-silent: The equals sign means the entire value, letter by letter. You are looking for a beginning, not a match.
why.h1-broken: The heading is no longer dark red.
blocks.0.text: The Bean Street page is finished. Someone wrote five rules for it — for the intro paragraph, the list, the note, the arrows and the outside link. Open the preview: not one of the five works. The console is clean. There is not a single warning.
blocks.2.text: Each of the five is written correctly. Braces closed, properties real, values sensible. That is why the browser says nothing — as far as it is concerned there is no problem. It looks for an element, finds none, and moves on. A rule that matches nothing is not an error to it.
blocks.3.text: A syntax error shouts at you. A selector that matches nothing says nothing.
blocks.4.text: How to hunt a silent rule
blocks.5.text: No tools, just reading. Four questions, in this order:
blocks.6.items.0: Does such an element exist at all? Open the HTML and look for the name. Letter by letter — capitals and small letters are different names.
blocks.6.items.1: Is the character right? A hash means id. A dot means class. The HTML says which one is there.
blocks.6.items.2: Is there a space where there should not be one? A space means inside. Without it both conditions describe the same element.
blocks.6.items.3: Is the value valid? An invalid value throws the whole declaration away without a word. Text needs quotes.
blocks.7.text: Every one of the five mistakes below you have already met in the last six lessons. Not one of them is new.
blocks.9.text: Fix the five rules in styles.css. Do not touch the HTML — it is correct. The intro paragraph becomes teal, the list saddlebrown, the note italic, an arrow appears before every item, and the outside link becomes bold.
